invalid_review_attempts.reason: Review returned two P2 findings (validator and unit test manifest fallback paths still referenced cp00-299 after the RP10 bootstrap rename) plus a P3 dangling README anchor; receipt gate requires P0=P1=P2=0. All three were remediated (cp00-299 -> cp00-321 in scripts/validate-rp10-intake-core-contract.mjs, packages/intake/test/model.test.js, packages/intake/src/service.js documentation_entry, contract regenerated) before the re-review.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-321 is a descriptor-only RP10 (Intake Conflict Engagement) bootstrap pack reviewed read-only in its pre-final-closeout state. The pack-level posture is honest and well-supported: manifest and command-evidence both declare production_ready=false with status implemented_pending_review and gate_outcome implementation_gates_passed_post_review_closeout_pending, so there is no over-claim of production readiness; the Hermes production_ready_candidate=true is merely coverage.valid && foundation.valid, not a production claim. The authority boundary is respected end-to-end: registry/service/validators/contract encode claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, and the manifest restates claude_not_final_approver=true and enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation=true. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in fact, not just asserted: a grep of packages/intake/src found no writeFile/appendFile/child_process/fetch/db/object-storage calls (only descriptor booleans set false), the frozen no_write_attestation pins every runtime/write/storage/Hermes-receipt flag to false, and fixtures carry fixture_payload_included=false/real_client_data_loaded=false. Hermes patterns are used only as deterministic evidence descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; authority boundary hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), not product runtime. Coverage reconciles cleanly and matches the baseline: 150 units across 20 descriptor sections (P00=52 + P01=98), deliverable distribution implementation:104/contract:3/security_audit:6/ui:19/fixture:3/test:9/hermes_evidence:3/claude_review:3 and the micro-title distribution both sum to 150, and CP00-321 is present in the live closeout-pack-plan.json (line 409). The three attempt-01 findings (two P2 + one P3 cp00-299 copy-paste artifacts) are independently verified as fully remediated — zero '299' references remain in scripts/validate-rp10-intake-core-contract.mjs, packages/intake/**, or contracts/intake-core-contract.json, and service.js documentation_entry plus its mirrored contract field both read #cp00-321. attempt-01 is correctly preserved as a not-accepted attempt under artifacts/.../attempt-01/ and documented in the manifest. The readiness auth-preflight passed with a non-empty valid-JSON result, and this review used only Read/Grep/Glob. Only two P3 issues were found, neither affecting gates, security, coverage, or boundaries: (1) validators.js:210 says 'twenty-one descriptor sections' but the pack defines twenty — a cosmetic wording carryover from the CP00-299 template where the identical issue was adjudicated P3, and which attempt-01 did not catch; and (2) command-evidence.json review_hardening.invalid_attempts_preserved is empty while the manifest preserves attempt-01 (likely a pre-review snapshot, flagged for reconciliation). No P0/P1/P2; the receipt gate (P0=P1=P2=0) is satisfied and the pack does not weaken any production_ready gate. Final enterprise-trust approval remains with the human approver — this review is advisory input only.

findings.title: Claude review packet question text says 'twenty-one' descriptor sections but the pack defines twenty
findings.details: The createIntakeCoreCp321ClaudeReviewPacket questions array states 'the twenty-one descriptor sections (P00 M00-M10 scope foundation cycle and P01 M00-M08 model foundation cycle ...)'. P00 M00-M10 is 11 sections and P01 M00-M08 is 9 sections, totaling twenty, not twenty-one. This contradicts the actual structure: INTAKE_CORE_CP321_REQUIREMENTS.required_section_rows has 20 keys, caseSet.section_count is 20 (asserted in packages/intake/test/model.test.js and the rp10 validator), and packages/intake/README.md says 'twenty descriptor-only sections'. The string is a human-readable question only and is NOT part of the contract-embedded scope_contract_foundation_descriptor (the questions array is excluded from the descriptor and the contract deepEqual), so it has no effect on coverage validation, the production_ready candidate, the contract-vs-descriptor deepEqual, or any no-write/boundary check. It is a copy-paste carryover from the CP00-299 crm-core template, where the identical wording was flagged as CP00-299-P3-01 and accepted as a non-blocking P3 (packages/crm/src/validators.js:509 still carries it). This pack's attempt-01 review did not flag it.
findings.recommendation: Change 'twenty-one descriptor sections' to 'twenty descriptor sections' in packages/intake/src/validators.js:210 to match the actual section count, the README, and the section_count assertions. No contract regeneration is required because the questions text is not mirrored into contracts/intake-core-contract.json.

findings.title: command-evidence review_hardening.invalid_attempts_preserved is empty while the manifest preserves attempt-01
findings.details: command-evidence.json review_hardening.invalid_attempts_preserved is [], but docs/closeout-packs/cp00-321/manifest.json records attempt-01 in both review_hardening.invalid_attempts_preserved (line 6337) and pack_level_claude_review.invalid_review_attempts (line 6314) — the not-accepted review whose two P2 cp00-299 manifest-fallback findings and one P3 anchor finding were remediated before re-review. The command-evidence block reads as a pre-review snapshot (its notes describe commands run 'before the pack-level Claude review run'), so the empty array may be an intentional point-in-time artifact rather than a defect; the manifest is the authoritative record and is correct. No gate, security, coverage, or boundary impact; flagged only so the two evidence files agree on the preserved attempt.
findings.recommendation: If command-evidence.json is regenerated at closeout, populate invalid_attempts_preserved with artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-321/attempt-01/ to match the manifest, or explicitly note that this block is a pre-review snapshot.
